data.text: I found two reusable patterns: repo-local `git remote get-url origin` helpers and a short `sha1` suffix convention already used elsewhere. I’m going to implement `ddxroot` with those same mechanics and keep the public surface small.

Plan:
1. Implement convention-mode resolution in `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go`, including XDG root selection and project-identity parsing.
2. Add the three requested tests in `cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go`, using real temp git repos for the path assertions.
3. Run the targeted package tests and the bead’s repo-level verification commands before committing.

I’ve narrowed the edit to `cli/internal/ddxroot`. I’m patching the package to resolve either in-tree `.ddx` or the XDG convention path, then I’ll add the three bead-specific tests around that behavior.
The implementation and test slice are in place. I’m formatting `ddxroot`, then running the targeted package tests to catch any parsing edge cases before the repo-wide gate.

data.text:
Implemented convention-mode `ddxroot` resolution in [ddxroot.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-fa6eedcc-20260515T053424-4c9c3bbc/cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot.go) so `Path` now keeps in-tree `.ddx/` when present, otherwise derives an XDG project root from the `origin` remote and falls back to `local/<basename>-<sha>` when no remote exists. I also added the requested coverage in [ddxroot_test.go](/var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-fa6eedcc-20260515T053424-4c9c3bbc/cli/internal/ddxroot/ddxroot_test.go), including the XDG github path assertion, the no-remote local fallback, and a table-driven URL parsing test for https, ssh, custom-host, and missing-remote cases.

Verification passed with `cd cli && go test ./internal/ddxroot/...` and `cd cli && lefthook run pre-commit`. Commit: `914fd5e38` (`refactor: add convention-mode ddxroot resolution [ddx-fa6eedcc]`).
